extension.valueMarkdown:
After the third loss, her OB/GYN suspects antiphospholipid syndrome (APS) based on the pattern of recurrent early losses with a chromosomally normal conceptus. She orders anticardiolipin antibodies, lupus anticoagulant, and anti-beta-2 glycoprotein I antibodies. Anticardiolipin IgG is elevated (48 GPL, positive >40) and lupus anticoagulant is weakly positive. These need to be confirmed on repeat testing 12 weeks apart per revised Sapporo criteria.

Her OB/GYN refers Lisa to: 1. Maternal-fetal medicine (MFM) for high-risk pregnancy planning and management 2. Hematology for APS evaluation and anticoagulation planning for future pregnancies

Both specialists need access to Lisa's complete OB/GYN records — the pregnancy loss history, prior workup results, and APS lab results — to provide consultation.

resource.conclusion: Serial beta-hCG levels demonstrate appropriate doubling (1,240 to 2,680 mIU/mL over 48 hours, doubling time ~43 hours), consistent with viable early intrauterine pregnancy.

coding.display: Choriogonadotropin.beta subunit [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma

resource.conclusion: Serial beta-hCG levels demonstrate decline from 4,120 to 3,480 mIU/mL over 48 hours, confirming non-viable pregnancy. Follow-up level of <5 mIU/mL on 2024-06-14 confirms complete resolution.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

resource.conclusion: Gestational sac identified measuring consistent with 5 weeks and 6 days. No yolk sac or fetal pole visualized. No adnexal masses. No free fluid in pelvis. Findings concerning for early pregnancy failure.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

coding.display: Choriogonadotropin.beta subunit [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma

resource.conclusion: Initial beta-hCG of 2,890 mIU/mL with appropriate 48-hour doubling to 6,210 mIU/mL. Despite reassuring initial kinetics, embryonic demise was confirmed on ultrasound. Follow-up beta-hCG of <5 mIU/mL on 2024-11-04 confirms complete resolution.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

resource.conclusion: Intrauterine gestational sac identified with yolk sac present. Fetal pole small for dates. No cardiac activity detected. No adnexal masses or free fluid. Findings concerning for possible early embryonic demise versus early dating; repeat scan recommended in one week.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

resource.conclusion: Fetal pole has not grown since prior scan (2024-10-14). Crown-rump length consistent with 6 weeks 2 days, indicating growth arrest. No cardiac activity detected. Findings consistent with embryonic demise (missed abortion).

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

resource.conclusion: ANA negative (titer <1:40). Reduces suspicion for systemic autoimmune contribution to recurrent pregnancy loss.

resource.conclusion: 46,XY (normal male karyotype). No balanced translocations detected. Rules out paternal chromosomal rearrangement as a cause of recurrent pregnancy loss.

resource.conclusion: Single intrauterine gestational sac with fetal pole identified. Fetal cardiac activity present, FHR 112 bpm. Crown-rump length 4.2 mm, consistent with 6 weeks 1 day gestational age. No adnexal masses or free fluid. Reassuring findings in setting of recurrent early pregnancy loss.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

resource.conclusion: Intrauterine gestational sac present. Fetal pole measuring 7 weeks 1 day with no interval growth from expected 8-week trajectory. Absent cardiac activity. Findings consistent with missed abortion; embryonic demise estimated to have occurred 1-2 weeks prior.

code.text: Early intrauterine pregnancy, approximately 4 weeks 5 days gestation by last menstrual period

note.text: Confirmed by positive serum beta-hCG with appropriate 48-hour doubling kinetics; estimated due date 2025-01-03
Pregnancy ended in first-trimester miscarriage at approximately 6 weeks gestation on 2024-06-03. Complete resolution confirmed by beta-hCG <5 mIU/mL on 2024-06-14.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

note.text: Confirmed by declining serial beta-hCG levels and transvaginal ultrasound showing gestational sac without embryonic structures at 6 weeks gestation. First pregnancy loss. Complete resolution confirmed by beta-hCG <5 mIU/mL on 2024-06-14.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

note.text: Second consecutive first-trimester pregnancy loss. Embryonic demise confirmed by absent growth and absent cardiac activity on serial transvaginal ultrasounds (6-week and 7-week scans). CRL consistent with growth arrest at approximately 6 weeks 2 days. Patient elected expectant management; miscarriage completed spontaneously over approximately 10 days. Beta-hCG confirmed complete resolution (<5 mIU/mL) on 2024-11-04.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

note.text: Three consecutive first-trimester losses (June 2024, October 2024, March 2025). Third loss confirmed euploid conceptus (46,XX) on POC cytogenetic analysis, eliminating embryonic aneuploidy as the cause and shifting suspicion toward a maternal factor. Prior RPL workup (parental karyotypes, TSH, HbA1c, coagulation, ANA) unrevealing. APS antibody panel planned.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

note.text: PHQ-2 positive at 3. Patient endorses persistent worry and difficulty sleeping related to pregnancy anxiety in context of two prior first-trimester losses. Currently engaged with therapist through EAP. Will monitor.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity
mental health information sensitivity

detail.display: 6-week transvaginal ultrasound with fetal cardiac activity

note.text: Third pregnancy (G3P0) confirmed at approximately 5 weeks gestation by positive serum beta-hCG with appropriate 48-hour doubling. 6-week transvaginal ultrasound showed fetal pole with cardiac activity (FHR 112 bpm) and CRL 4.2 mm consistent with 6 weeks 1 day. Pregnancy ended as missed abortion diagnosed 2025-03-10 at 8-week follow-up — absent cardiac activity and growth arrest at 7w1d. D&C performed 2025-03-12.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

detail.display: 8-week transvaginal ultrasound showing absent cardiac activity

note.text: Third consecutive first-trimester pregnancy loss. Fetal pole growth arrest at approximately 7 weeks 1 day with absent cardiac activity on 8-week follow-up transvaginal ultrasound. D&C performed 2025-03-12. Cytogenetic analysis of POC revealed normal female karyotype (46,XX), eliminating embryonic aneuploidy as the cause.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

note.text: Positive PHQ-2 screen (score 4). Significant anxiety and depressed mood related to recurrent losses. Currently engaged in therapy through EAP. Recommended continuation of therapy and formal mental health referral if symptoms worsen.

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa presented as a new patient after relocating to Denver from Portland, Oregon. She was 33 years old and in excellent general health with no significant past medical history, no prior surgeries, and no known drug allergies (NKDA documented).

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa and her husband Ryan (married 4 years) presented for a preconception counseling visit. She expressed readiness to begin trying to conceive.

reasonCode.text: First pregnancy confirmation and early monitoring

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa presented with a positive home pregnancy test at approximately 4 weeks and 5 days by LMP (last menstrual period March 29, 2024). She was excited and anxious.

reasonCode.text: First pregnancy loss — vaginal bleeding and cramping at approximately 6 weeks gestation

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa presented urgently at approximately 6 weeks gestation with lower abdominal cramping and vaginal spotting that had started the previous day and progressed to moderate bleeding.

reasonCode.text: Second pregnancy confirmation, early pregnancy monitoring, and pregnancy loss (missed abortion)

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa conceived again in September 2024 and presented at approximately 5 weeks gestation for early confirmation. She was understandably anxious given her prior loss.

reasonCode.text: Recurrent pregnancy loss workup - blood draw and results counseling

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa presented for blood draw and counseling related to the recurrent pregnancy loss workup. Ryan accompanied her for parental karyotyping.

reasonCode.text: Third pregnancy confirmation and early monitoring

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa conceived a third time in January 2025 and presented at approximately 5 weeks gestation. She was anxious from the start, and Dr.

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa presented for her scheduled 8-week follow-up ultrasound. She reported that her nausea and breast tenderness had diminished over the past week, raising her concern.

reasonCode.text: APS antibody panel results review, counseling, and specialist referrals

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

extension.valueMarkdown: Lisa returned for the APS antibody panel blood draw and comprehensive counseling. She was accompanied by Ryan.

extension.valueMarkdown: Dr. Forsythe called Lisa to confirm that both specialist referral appointments had been scheduled: MFM consultation with Dr.

location.display: Rocky Mountain Women's Health affiliated ambulatory surgery center

outcome.text: Uncomplicated. Products of conception obtained and sent for histopathology and cytogenetic karyotype analysis.

report.display: Cytogenetic karyotype analysis of products of conception

note.text: D&C performed to expedite physical recovery and obtain products of conception for cytogenetic analysis. Procedure uncomplicated.

code.text: Referral to UC Health Hematology for APS diagnostic confirmation and thrombophilia screening

reasonCode.text: Elevated anticardiolipin IgG, weakly positive lupus anticoagulant

note.text: Referral to Dr. James Okafor, Hematology (NPI: 1234567893), UC Health Hematology, for APS diagnostic confirmation, thrombophilia screening, and anticoagulation planning. Attached: APS antibody results, coagulation studies, pregnancy loss history, RPL workup.

security.display: sexuality and reproductive health information sensitivity

code.text: Referral to Colorado Maternal-Fetal Medicine for high-risk pregnancy planning

note.text: Referral to Dr. Sarah Whitfield, MFM (NPI: 1467823159), Colorado Maternal-Fetal Medicine, for high-risk pregnancy planning in the setting of suspected APS. Attached: pregnancy loss history, RPL workup results, APS antibody panel, POC cytogenetics report.
